# services.crowdsec-firewall-bouncer

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.crowdsec-firewall-bouncer`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.crowdsec-firewall-bouncer.createRulesets` | `boolean` | Whether to have the module create the appropriate firewall configuration based on the bouncer settings. You may disable this option to manually configure it. |
| `services.crowdsec-firewall-bouncer.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable CrowdSec Firewall Bouncer. |
| `services.crowdsec-firewall-bouncer.package` | `package` | The crowdsec-firewall-bouncer package to use. |
| `services.crowdsec-firewall-bouncer.registerBouncer.bouncerName` | `non-empty string` | Name to register the bouncer as to the CrowdSec API |
| `services.crowdsec-firewall-bouncer.registerBouncer.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to automatically register the bouncer to the locally running `crowdsec` service. When authenticating to an external CrowdSec API, you may use the [](#opt-services.crowdsec-firewall-bouncer.secrets.apiKeyPath) option instead. |
| `services.crowdsec-firewall-bouncer.secrets.apiKeyPath` | `null or absolute path` | Path to the API key to authenticate with a local CrowdSec API. You need to call `cscli bouncers add <bouncer-name>` to register the bouncer and get this API key. When authenticating to the locally running `crowdsec` service, you may use the [](#opt-services.crowdsec-firewall-bouncer.registerBouncer.enable) option instead. |
| `services.crowdsec-firewall-bouncer.settings` | `open submodule of (YAML 1.1 value)` | Settings for the main CrowdSec Firewall Bouncer. Refer to the defaults at <https://github.com/crowdsecurity/cs-firewall-bouncer/blob/main/config/crowdsec-firewall-bouncer.yaml>. |
| `services.crowdsec-firewall-bouncer.settings.api_key` | `null or string` | API key to authenticate with a local crowdsec API. You need to call `cscli bouncers add <bouncer-name>` to register the bouncer and get this API key. Setting this option will store this secret in the Nix store. Instead, you should set the `services.crowdsec-firewall-bouncer.secrets.apiKeyPath` option, which will read the value at runtime. |
| `services.crowdsec-firewall-bouncer.settings.api_url` | `string` | URL of the local API. |
| `services.crowdsec-firewall-bouncer.settings.mode` | `string` | Firewall mode to use. |
